questions.question: Define argc and argv in the context of C++ main function. What does each represent?
Write a C++ program that accepts two integers as command line arguments and displays their sum.
keyPoints: Handle errors with try-catch or validation

questions.question: Explain why it is important to validate command line arguments before using them.
What is the difference between stoi() and atoi() functions for converting strings to integers?
keyPoints: stoi() throws exception on invalid input

questions.question: How do you pass an argument containing spaces from the command line? Give an example.
keyPoints: Use double quotes around the argument
Without quotes, Hello and World become separate arguments
Single quotes work in some shells too

questions.question: Explain the concept of options/flags in command line programs with an example.
What is argv[argc] and what is its purpose?
keyPoints: It marks the end of the argument array
Never access it as a valid argument

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program that accepts a filename as command line argument, opens the file, and displays its contents. Include proper error handling and usage message.
keyPoints: Include necessary headers: iostream, fstream, string
Include usage message for wrong arguments

questions.question: Explain the complete flow of command line arguments from when a user types a command in the terminal until the main() function receives them. Discuss how the OS and runtime environment are involved.
keyPoints: Sets up argc and argv before main()
Runtime calls main() with these parameters

questions.question: Write a C++ program that accepts multiple command line options: -a (add), -s (subtract), -m (multiply), and takes two numbers as positional arguments. Perform the operation based on the option provided and handle all error cases appropriately.

Create a utility program that accepts a filename and an optional -v (verbose) flag. The program should count and display the number of lines, words, and characters in the file. When -v is enabled, display additional information.
keyPoints: If verbose, show filename and extra details
